query_normalized: predicting the yield strength of steel alloys from composition and heat treatment parameters materials science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (b) because it investigates mechanical strength and constitutive behavior in high-chromium martensitic steel, which falls within the user's domain of steel alloys and addresses the same dependent variable category (strength/yield behavior). Additionally, it explores process-structure-property relationships using symbolic regression, providing relevant methodological context for predicting material properties from processing conditions.
